title: Bolivia Considers Adding Tether's USDT to National Payments System
summary: Bolivia is exploring the integration of Tether's USDT stablecoin into its national payments system. This move comes after crypto transaction volumes surged to $430 million in the year following the central bank's removal of restrictions in mid-2024.
body:
Bolivia is considering the inclusion of Tether's USDT stablecoin in its national payments system, according to reports. This potential move is part of a broader trend of increasing crypto adoption in the country, which has seen a significant uptick in transaction volumes.

The decision to explore USDT integration follows a surge in crypto usage in Bolivia. Transaction volumes reached $430 million in the year after the central bank removed restrictions on cryptocurrency transactions in mid-2024. This development highlights the growing acceptance of digital assets in the country's financial ecosystem.

For everyday people, this could mean easier access to stablecoin transactions, potentially reducing reliance on traditional banking systems. It may also provide a more stable alternative to the Bolivian boliviano, especially in times of economic uncertainty.

The next steps will likely involve further discussions and regulatory frameworks to ensure the smooth integration of USDT into the national payments system. Users should watch for official announcements from the Bolivian government and central bank on this matter.

Watch for official announcements from the Bolivian government on this development. Could mean easier access to stablecoin transactions for everyday users. Read more → https://www.cryptocatalyst.news/articles/2026-07-13-bolivia-considers-adding-tethers-usdt-to-national-payments-system
